document.id: Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa Indians Res. No. 512-07-25
document.codify:doc: Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa Indians Tribal Code
document.heading: Resolution Number TMBC512-07-25 of the Duly Elected and Certified Governing Body of the Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa Indians

citations.citation: Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa Indians Res. No. 512-07-25

recital.text: the Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa Indians, hereinafter referred to as the Tribe, is an unincorporated Band acting under a revised Constitution and By-Laws approved by the Secretary of the Interior on June 16th, 1959 and amendments thereto approved; and

Article IX (a) Section 1 of the Turtle Mountain Constitution and By-Laws empowers the Tribal Council with the authority to represent the Band and to negotiate with the Federal, State, and Local Governments and with private persons; and

Article IX (a) Section 1 of the Turtle Mountain Constitution and Bylaws requires a 30-day comment period prior to the adoption of any ordinances or amendments to the Tribal Code, whether proposed by resolution or otherwise. Adoption must occur through a roll call vote of the Tribal Council at a publicly held meeting; and,
the Tribe is proposing to amend Title 19, Alcoholic Beverage Controls to remove the word “tap” referring to the fees for Specialty Licenses; and
by the adoption of TMBC335-04-25, the proposed amendment to Title 19 was placed out for public comment and no comments were received; and

that the Tribe is approving the amendment to Title 19.01.050 (e) to remove the word “tap” when referring to the fees for Specialty License; class of: (e) Specialty license at an annual fee of four hundred dollars ($400) for beer, and five hundred dollars ($500) per year to sell beer and wine; now
that Title 19 of the Tribal Code will be codified accordingly.

procedural-data.text: I, the undersigned Tribal Secretary of the Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa Indians, do hereby certify that the Tribal Council is composed of nine (9) members of whom seven (7) constituting a quorum were present at a meeting duly called, convened and held on the 24th day of July, 2025 that the foregoing resolution was adopted by an affirmative vote of six (6) in favor - Representatives Craig Lunday, Ron Trottier Sr., Chad Counts, Blaine “Slugger” Davis, Kenneth Malaterre and Lynn Gourneau; two (2) absent- Reps. Jon Jon Keplin and Elmer Davis Jr.; none (0) opposed; with the Tribal Chairman not voting.
( ) SIGNED INTO LAW / Dated the ____th day of ___________, 2024

codify:replace.codified:doc: Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa Indians Tribal Code

para.text: Specialty license at an annual fee of four hundred dollars ($400) for beer, and five hundred dollars ($500) per year to sell beer and wine.
